ops.title: ccx vcs ship can die on .git/index.lock (auto-sync hook race) AFTER committing — check main@origin first (push usually succeeded, only the CI watch was lost); if main truly lags, jj bookmark move + jj git push; never re-run ship

ops.body:
In cc-notes (2026-07-12), `ccx vcs ship` failed with "Could not acquire lock … .git/index.lock" during its advance-bookmark step — the capt-hook auto-sync fired concurrently on the commit. The commit itself landed (jj `@-`), but the `main` bookmark move was rolled back, so `jj git push` reported "Nothing changed".

**Why:** ship = commit → advance bookmark → push → CI watch; a step-3 lock collision leaves state committed-but-unpushed with the bookmark still on the old tip.

**How to apply:** don't re-run ship (it would try to commit again). First check whether the push actually landed: `jj git fetch` then compare `main@origin` — in the common variant (cc-skills, twice on 2026-07-13) the push SUCCEEDED and only the post-push "Failed to reset Git HEAD state" step died, so nothing needs re-pushing; only the ship's CI watch was lost — re-arm it by commit. If main truly lags `@-` (the rollback variant), run `jj bookmark move main --to <change>` then `jj git push --bookmark main` (a plain `jj git push` says "Nothing changed" when `@` is an empty working copy and the bookmark wasn't advanced — move the bookmark first). Then watch CI by commit (`gh run list --commit <sha>` — [[gh-run-watch-target-by-workflow-and-sha]]). The lock is transient — verify `.git/index.lock` is gone rather than deleting blindly. Related: [[verify-lineage-before-ship]], [[jj-colocated-use-jj-not-git-stash]].
